message:
feat(vendored/mux_pool): tick 155 4 cx-first pool siblings + return_client routing (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Sixth post-storage tick. vendored/mux_pool.rs had 9 `_with_cx`
methods; naive scan flagged 6 missing. Two were non-asupersync-only
(`acquire_client_inner`, `execute_with_recovery_inner`), leaving
4 real targets.

New siblings (all thin wrappers routing through the underlying
ConnectionPool cx-first methods):
- return_client_with_cx — routes through `pool.put_with_cx` so
  a cancelled caller interrupts the idle-pool mutex acquire
  rather than waiting.
- evict_idle_with_cx — routes through `pool.evict_idle_with_cx`
  so periodic-eviction tasks can be interrupted by shutdown
  cancellation without hanging on the idle mutex.
- clear_with_cx — routes through `pool.clear_with_cx` so
  shutdown-flush paths bail under a cancelled parent cx instead
  of deadlocking.
- stats_with_cx — routes through `pool.stats_with_cx` so a
  telemetry exporter running under a cancelled parent doesn't
  stall on the stats snapshot.

Each sibling uses tracing with `explicit_cx = true` and a
"(cx path)" suffix so log greps can distinguish the code path
that produced the event from the legacy ambient-cx path.

Call-site migration in `execute_with_recovery_with_cx` (L335):
- Line 358 (was `self.return_client(client)`) →
  `self.return_client_with_cx(cx, client)`

The corresponding call in `execute_with_recovery_inner` (the
non-asupersync path at L417) is unchanged. Test-only call at
L1850 also unchanged — uses ambient helpers.

Verified via fork bypass: 92/92 vendored::mux_pool:: tests pass,
including the full health-check-with-cx suite and pool recovery
regression gates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
